summary: Completed/matched Mario fighter code by replacing the old single ftmario.c and a large portion of ftmario.s with five C translation units, expanding ftmario.h declarations/attributes, and renaming Mario symbols to the ftMario_func_800E#### convention across data tables and cross-character callers. The PR had no body or review comments in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from code movement and build-table updates.
what_changed: Deleted the old 552-line src/melee/ft/chara/ftMario/ftmario.c and added five new ftmario1.c through ftmario5.c files totaling about 989 added C lines.
Removed about 1238 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s, leaving mostly data references that now point at C-defined ftMario_func_800E#### symbols.

Expanded ftMarioAttributes in ftmario.h from padding-heavy layout to named offset fields including x18 through x5C, added ftMarioUnkStruct, included ftcommon.h, and added a GetMarioAttr inline helper.
Renamed Mario function declarations from generic func_800E#### names to ftMario_func_800E#### in ftmario.h, include/functions.h, ftDrMario C code, ftdrmario.s, ftdata.s, and assembly callsites.
Updated obj_files.mk to compile ftmario1.c.o through ftmario5.c.o instead of the old ftmario.c.o.
Ported Mario on-load/on-death and shared setup code, item-related logic, fireball/cape/tornado-like state handlers, action-state transitions, callbacks, and special-attribute access into C.
Updated Dr. Mario code and assembly tables to call shared Mario routines such as ftMario_func_800E0BE4 and ftMario_func_800E0D1C rather than unscoped generic symbols.
Updated item and fighter assembly callsites in asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s and asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s to branch to newly named ftMario functions.
smart_moves: Split a large fighter file into numbered translation units, then mirrored that split in header comment sections and obj_files.mk; this makes ordering and matching work more manageable for a large character module.
Kept address-based function names but prefixed them with ftMario_, preserving searchability by address while avoiding ambiguous global func_800E#### names that are shared-looking across fighters.
Centralized special-attribute access through GetMarioAttr while still preserving exact offset names in ftMarioAttributes for matching.
Used small helper functions for repeated transition/callback setup, such as _ftMario_call_FASC_and_set_vars, _ftMario_check_223C_and_set, and helpers shared by pairs of nearly identical routines.
Propagated symbol renames into action-state tables and external assembly references in the same PR, reducing the risk of stale relocations or mismatched function-pointer tables.
Preserved matching-oriented artifacts where needed, including explicit padding arrays, macros for constants such as PI_3, DEGREES_TO_RADIANS, and HALF_PI, and decomp.me scratch links in comments.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a fighter module, update all consumers of the old symbols, not just the new C files: action-state tables in ftdata.s and character-specific assembly data can hold many function pointers.
Character code may be shared by clone characters; Dr. Mario called Mario routines, so namespacing Mario symbols required updates in ftDrMario sources and ftdrmario.s as well.
Expanding an attributes struct from padding into offset-annotated fields gives later functions readable field access without losing ABI/layout control.
For large character completions, splitting C into numbered chunks can align with build/link order and make it easier to replace only the matching assembly ranges.
Matching sometimes benefits from local helpers and inlines that express repeated compiler output patterns while keeping emitted code equivalent.
Keep address suffixes in provisional symbol names until semantic names are known; they remain easy to cross-reference against asm, data tables, and decomp.me scratches.
naming_conventions: Mario functions were renamed from func_800E#### to ftMario_func_800E####.
New source files use lowercase numbered names: ftmario1.c, ftmario2.c, ftmario3.c, ftmario4.c, ftmario5.c.
Header sections group declarations by file using comments like //ftmario1 through //ftmario5.
Private/local helpers use leading underscores and descriptive pairing names, for example _ftMario_800E207C_800E2194_helper and _ftMario_func_800E1BE4_and_800E1D2C.
Special-attribute fields retain offset-style names such as x18, x1C, x50, and x5C rather than invented semantic names.
The inline special-attribute accessor is named GetMarioAttr and returns ftMarioAttributes* from Fighter.x2D4_specialAttributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly function bodies in ftmario.s with C equivalents while leaving data labels/function-pointer tables that refer to the new C symbols.
Changed .4byte entries in ftmario.s, ftdrmario.s, and ftdata.s from generic func_800E#### pointers to ftMario_func_800E#### pointers.
Updated branch targets in assembly callsites, including code_800877F8.s and code_8027CF30.s, to call ftMario_func_800E0D1C, ftMario_func_800E132C, and ftMario_func_800E1428.
Used explicit action-state IDs and flags such as 0x157, 0x158, 0x159, 0x15A, 0x15B, 0x15C, 0x15D, 0x15E, 0x0C4C508A, and 0x0C4C508C to preserve known binary behavior.
Used padding arrays in functions and structs to influence stack/layout matching where needed.
Used macros and exact double/float constants for codegen-sensitive math paths, for example PI_3, DEGREES_TO_RADIANS, HALF_PI, and an abs macro.
Several functions include decomp.me scratch URLs in comments, indicating isolated matching work for difficult routines.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftMario_func_800E#### functions to semantic Mario move names.
Search for follow-up fixes touching ftMarioAttributes offsets x18 through x5C.
Search for PRs that further decompile or rename Dr. Mario callsites depending on shared Mario functions.
Find later cleanup of duplicated ftMario_func_800E13F8 declaration in ftmario.h.
Search for other fighter completions that split one character file into numbered translation units and update obj_files.mk.
